headline: Mission: Impossible - Ghost Protocol Sparks Streaming Revival, Proving Cruise's Timeless Action Magnetism
lede: Fifteen years after its groundbreaking theatrical release, Brad Bird's franchise-reinventing spy thriller continues to captivate audiences through strategic streaming availability.
body:
The fourth installment in the Mission: Impossible franchise remains a pivotal moment in Tom Cruise's career trajectory, demonstrating the actor's unparalleled commitment to practical stunt work and cinematic spectacle.

Released in 2011, *Ghost Protocol* represented a critical pivot point for the spy franchise. Director Brad Bird, making his live-action debut after animation triumphs like *The Incredibles*, infused the film with kinetic energy and visual inventiveness that redefined the series' aesthetic.

The film's most iconic sequence — Cruise's death-defying Burj Khalifa climb in Dubai — remains a watershed moment in modern action cinema. Performed entirely without CGI, the sequence epitomized Cruise's legendary dedication to authentic stunt performance, a trademark that has distinguished his action portfolio.

Streaming accessibility has reignited audience interest, introducing a new generation to the film's innovative set pieces and Cruise's magnetic screen presence. The movie's technical precision and pulse-pounding sequences continue to influence contemporary action filmmaking.

Critically, *Ghost Protocol* represents more than a standalone thriller. It served as a franchise resurrection, rehabilitating the Mission: Impossible brand after the relative disappointment of its third installment and establishing the template for subsequent high-octane entries.
key_facts: Burj Khalifa sequence performed without CGI
Cruise performed his own extreme stunts
